fix: cross-target path mapping for skills/agents in `apm pack` (#426)

* Initial plan

* fix: cross-target path mapping for skills/agents in apm pack

When skills are installed to .github/skills/ but pack targets claude,
the packer now remaps those paths to .claude/skills/ in the bundle.
Only skills/ and agents/ are mapped (commands, instructions, hooks
are target-specific). Adds mapped_count and path_mappings to PackResult,
records mapped_from in enriched lockfile pack section.

commit.message:
fix(server): clear is_fall_flying when player lands

Vanilla ends elytra flight when the player touches the ground.
Previously the flag was only set on StartFallFlying but never
cleared, causing permanent elytra speed-check bypass.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: Oxidized
repository.description: A high-performance Minecraft Java Edition server rewritten in Rust. Targets Minecraft 26.1 — the first fully unobfuscated release.

commit.message:
feat(server): add block interaction validation for build height, spawn protection, and replacement

Add three vanilla-compliant validations to block breaking and placement:

1. Build height validation: reject block actions outside overworld limits
   (y < -64 or y > 319) with acknowledgement but no state change.

2. Spawn protection enforcement: non-operator players cannot break or place
   blocks within spawn_protection radius (Chebyshev distance) of the world
   spawn. Configurable via gameplay.spawn_protection (default: 16, 0 disables).

3. Block replacement validation: placement only succeeds if the target
   position contains air (state ID 0). Non-air positions trigger a resync
   to correct client prediction.

Add spawn_protection field to ServerContext, populated from
GameplayConfig at startup. Include 13 new unit tests covering all three
validation paths.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: Oxidized
repository.description: A high-performance Minecraft Java Edition server rewritten in Rust. Targets Minecraft 26.1 — the first fully unobfuscated release.

commit.message:
fix(server): add config timeout, teleport resend, and time broadcast clock

- Configuration phase: add 30s timeout to read_raw_packet() calls to
  prevent malicious clients from holding connections open indefinitely
- Teleport resend: track sent time in pending_teleports and resend
  unconfirmed teleports every 1 second (vanilla: 20 ticks) in the play
  tick timer
- Time broadcast: include overworld ClockUpdate in periodic
  ClientboundSetTimePacket so clients synchronise day/night cycle
- Update pending_teleports type from (i32, Vec3) to (i32, Vec3, Instant)
  across all call sites and tests

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: Oxidized
repository.description: A high-performance Minecraft Java Edition server rewritten in Rust. Targets Minecraft 26.1 — the first fully unobfuscated release.

commit.message:
fix(server): consult gamerules for movement validation

Read PlayerMovementCheck and ElytraMovementCheck game rules in the
movement handler and skip speed validation when they are disabled,
matching vanilla ServerGamePacketListenerImpl behavior.

- Extract should_skip_speed_check() helper for testable logic
- PlayerMovementCheck=false skips all speed validation
- ElytraMovementCheck=false skips speed validation during elytra flight
- NaN/Infinity rejection and coordinate clamping are never skipped
- Add 7 unit tests covering all gamerule combinations

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(server): add vanilla-compliant chat input validation and suggestion cap

- Add character validation matching vanilla isChatMessageIllegal():
  reject control chars (0x00-0x1F) except tab and newline, plus § and DEL
- Add message length validation (max 256 chars, vanilla MAX_CHAT_LENGTH)
- Silently ignore empty chat messages (vanilla behaviour)
- Cap command suggestions at 1000 entries (vanilla MAX_COMMAND_SUGGESTIONS)
- Add 15 unit tests for validation functions

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.description: JS - Editor: VS Code in the browser for Synology NAS

commit.message:
fix(server): vanilla compliance — 8 behavioral bugs fixed

- Teleport confirmation: search full queue instead of front-only
  (vanilla removeIf behavior, later IDs implicitly confirm earlier)
- Health/food/saturation: clamp values on NBT load to valid ranges
- SpawnAngle: load and save from player NBT (was always defaulting 0.0)
- View distance: enforce minimum 2 chunks (vanilla clamps client value)
- Mining state: add dedicated mining_start_pos/time fields instead of
  corrupting spawn_pos for block break tracking
- Mining timing: reject StopDestroyBlock within 50ms of start (prevents
  instant-break exploit in survival mode)
- Elytra flight: handle StartFallFlying action in PlayerCommand handler
- View distance: use clamp(2, max) instead of just min(max)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(game): reject NaN/Infinity in creative mode movement validation

Creative/spectator mode was blindly overriding the entire movement
validation result, including NaN/Infinity rejections. Vanilla only
skips the speed-limit check for these game modes — containsInvalidValues
still applies.

Add `has_invalid_values` field to `MovementResult` so the server crate
can distinguish speed rejections (overridable) from NaN rejections (not
overridable). Update the creative/spectator bypass to check this flag.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

expected moves, and wrong-move hints. Total: 10 lessons.

feat(lessons): add click-to-answer interactivity to LessonView

LessonView now handles board clicks when a step has prompt/expectedMove.
Green flash on correct, red flash + hint on wrong. Respects acceptRadius
for fuzzy matching. branchOnFail redirects after 3 wrong attempts.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(store): add lesson interaction state for click-to-answer

Adds lessonInteraction slice with setLessonPrompt, checkLessonAnswer,
clearLessonPrompt actions. Supports exact and fuzzy (acceptRadius)
matching, attempt tracking, and feedback state.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

7 new features: volume control, rivalry system, challenges, leaderboard, streaming TTS, animations, hand wash

Features added:
- Volume/gain control: +/- keys, audio_gain config, visual bar overlay
- Guest rivalry system: cross-visitor opinion tracking, dramatic announcements
- 5 mini-games: Mario Trivia, Name That Character, Bathroom Dare, Story Builder, Would You Rather
- Party leaderboard: F6 overlay + leaderboard.html TV display page
- Sentence streaming TTS: play first sentence while rest generates
- Smooth animations: crossfade transitions, breathing, talking bounce, entrance/exit, excitement shake, emotion flash
- Enhanced hand wash reminders: 10 dramatic Neuro-sama style reminders, separate TTS on exit

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(lessons): add interactive step fields to LessonStep type

Add wrongMoveHint, branchOnFail, acceptRadius to support click-to-answer
lessons. Existing prompt/expectedMove fields are now complemented by
feedback and branching capabilities.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: sentence streaming TTS — play first sentence while rest synthesizes

- Add split_into_sentences() and synthesize_streaming() to server/tts.py
- Upgrade _generate_and_send_response() to split into N sentences and
  stream audio_chunk messages (was limited to 2-sentence split)
- Add audio_chunk WebSocket message type with chunk_index/total_chunks/is_last
- Client ws_client.py routes audio_chunk binary to new on_audio_chunk callback
- Client main.py queues streaming chunks sequentially, manages speaking state
  and echo cancellation across all chunks (only clears on final chunk)
- Config flag: tts_streaming (default true) gates the feature
- Debug logging: [DEBUG_STREAM] shows sentence-by-sentence progress
- Graceful fallback: if streaming fails, falls back to full synthesis
- Single-sentence responses skip streaming (use existing path)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Neuro-sama party overhaul: gossip system, chaos personality, emotion upgrades

- party_gossip.py: cross-visitor social dynamics, guest titles, rivalry detection
- mario_prompt.py: chaotic/opinionated/dramatic personality rewrite
- emotions.py: wider voice modulation, cheer-up system, faster decay
- main.py: gossip in idle loop, circuit breaker recovery, mood swings
- idle_behavior.py: 20 existential crisis / unhinged idle mumbles
- mario_display.py: 12 new emotion particles, keyword particle effects
- e2e_test.py: expanded test coverage

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: remove WIP folder contents from tracking and strengthen gitignore (#420)

The WIP/ directory was accidentally committed to main. Remove the
tracked file and update the gitignore entry from 'WIP' to 'WIP/' for
an explicit directory-only match, ensuring no WIP contents are ever
pushed regardless of filename.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

perf: replace framer-motion with CSS compositor animations for 60fps

Core change: move ALL user-facing animations from JS-driven (framer-motion
main thread) to CSS @keyframes (compositor thread, inherently 60fps).

Motion components rewritten:
- StaggerList/StaggerItem: CSS animation-delay via --stagger-delay custom prop
- TapCard/TapButton: pure CSS :active scale transform (no JS per-frame)
- FadeIn/PageTransition: CSS fade-in-up keyframe animation
- SlideIn: CSS animation instead of clipPath framer variant

DetailModal rewritten:
- Replaced framer-motion spring with CSS transition (transform 0.32s)
- Enter/exit via className toggle + requestAnimationFrame
- Content uses CSS scale-in keyframe

Other changes:
- PageHeader: CSS anim-fade-in instead of motion.h1/motion.p
- BottomBar: removed layoutId spring, pure CSS active indicator
- FilterBar: plain button with .tap-btn CSS class
 CSS anim classes

framer-motion now only loaded on admin dashboard pages (not user-facing).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

perf: optimize PWA for 60fps rendering - Replace backdrop-blur-xl with backdrop-blur-md on sticky/fixed elements (PageHeader, BottomBar, DetailModal sticky header) - Add GPU acceleration: will-change hints, translateZ(0) via .gpu-layer class - Fix SlideIn: replace height animation (layout thrashing) with clipPath - Remove whileHover y-translate on TapCard (triggers layout); keep scale-only - Replace all transition-all with specific properties (transition-colors, transition-shadow, transition-opacity, transition-transform) - Memoize DataProvider context value with useMemo to prevent cascading re-renders - Add React.memo to: EventCard, FilterBar, StaggerItem, TapCard, TapButton - Add useMemo for expensive computations (timeGroups, filtering, locations) - Hoist Intl.DateTimeFormat outside render loop (avoid per-frame allocation) - Optimize HapticProvider: passive pointerdown listener, cached selector - Add contain:content to .card-clean for layout containment - Add loading="lazy" to all tags - Add will-change: background-position to skeleton animation Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add complete Vue3 Web StarCraft game

- Vue3 + Vite + TypeScript + Pinia setup
- Canvas 2D cartoon-style renderer (tiles, units, buildings, resources)
- Web Audio API synthesized sound effects (no external files)
- Game engine: movement, combat, resource gathering, unit production
- Simple AI opponent (gather, produce, attack)
- 3 playable races: Terran, Zerg, Protoss with 3 units + 3 buildings each
- HUD: resource bar, command panel, train buttons
- Minimap with click-to-navigate
- WASD/Arrow keys + mouse wheel camera scroll
- Box selection + shift-click additive selection
- Victory/defeat conditions
- Dockerfile + nginx.conf + docker-compose.yml
- GitHub Actions CI/CD with permissions hardening and Telegram notifications via secrets

commit.message:
fix: show auth diagnostics in --verbose for virtual package validation (#414)

* fix: resolve both sides of relative_to() for Windows 8.3 path compat

On Windows CI runners, Path.resolve() expands 8.3 short names (RUNNER~1 →
runneradmin). If only base_dir is resolved but agents_path is not,
relative_to() raises ValueError and falls back to absolute paths.

Fix: call .resolve() on both sides of relative_to() in
_generate_placement_summary and _generate_distributed_summary.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: show auth diagnostics in --verbose for virtual package validation

When virtual package validation fails (subdirectory skills, files,
collections), running with --verbose now shows the same auth resolution
details and actionable error context that non-virtual packages already
showed. Previously, --verbose produced no additional output for virtual
packages despite the hint suggesting it would.

Also reuses the existing AuthResolver instance instead of creating a
new one in the downloader, ensuring consistent auth state.

Closes #413

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* refactor: use resolve_for_dep for virtual package auth resolution

Address review feedback: use AuthResolver.resolve_for_dep(dep_ref)
instead of manually extracting host/org, reducing drift between
the virtual and non-virtual validation paths.

Also assert resolve_for_dep is called in the test.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat(ci): add automated release pipeline with cross-platform binaries

Add release-please for automated version bumping and release PR management,
git-cliff for changelog generation, commitlint for conventional commit
enforcement, and GitHub Actions workflows for cross-platform binary builds.

Two release flows:
- Dev (nightly): rolling pre-release on every CI-passing push to main
- Stable: release-please creates Release PR, maintainer merges, binaries
  built for 5 targets (linux-gnu, linux-musl, windows, macos-x86, macos-arm)

New files:
- cliff.toml, release-please-config.json, .release-please-manifest.json
- .commitlintrc.yml
- .github/workflows/{release-please,release-binaries,dev-release,commit-lint}.yml
- docs/adr/adr-039-release-strategy.md

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Status handler:

Handshake:

Login:

- Add 30-second timeout on login packet reads

Movement:

- Skip speed validation for creative/spectator (keep sanitization)
- Store teleport position with ID for proper confirmation

Configuration:

fix(server): correct 4 movement handler bugs for vanilla compliance

1. Elytra state: read is_fall_flying from ServerPlayer instead of
   hardcoded false, enabling 300 block/tick limit during elytra flight.

2. Game mode bypass: skip speed validation for Creative/Spectator
   players (NaN/coordinate sanitization still applies).

3. Teleport position tracking: store (id, Vec3) tuples in
   pending_teleports so confirmations can verify target position.

   (6 per tick × 20 TPS) matching vanilla DoS protection.

commit.message:
feat(protocol): add swing, player abilities, and client info play packets

Add three missing serverbound packet handlers:

- ServerboundSwingPacket (0x3F): Broadcasts ClientboundAnimatePacket
  to other players when a player swings their arm.
- ServerboundPlayerAbilitiesPacket (0x28): Updates player flying state,
  gated by can_fly permission.
- ServerboundClientInformationPlayPacket (0x0E): Updates player view
  distance when settings change during play state.

Also adds ClientboundAnimatePacket (0x02) for broadcasting entity
animations. All packet IDs verified against 26.1-pre-3 packets.json.

commit.message:
fix(game): implement destroy/keep/replace modes for /setblock command

Previously all three modes executed identically, unconditionally replacing
the block. Now each mode has its own executor:

- replace (default): unconditionally replaces the block
- keep: only replaces if the existing block is air
- destroy: sets to air first (simulating breaking), then places new block

Added get_block() to ServerHandle trait and implemented it in ServerContext
to support querying the existing block state for keep mode.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

perf(nbt): avoid cloning NbtCompound in from_compound()

Add CompoundDeserializer that borrows the NbtCompound directly instead
of cloning it into an NbtTag::Compound wrapper. The previous code
cloned the entire compound tree just so NbtDeserializer could unwrap
the reference — this new path skips the allocation entirely.

commit.message:
fix(server): vanilla compliance audit — 7 protocol and behavior fixes

- Fix is_flat flag: propagate generator type to login packet (BUG-01)
- Remove spurious ChangeGameMode event during login (BUG-02)
- Reorder login sequence to match vanilla PlayerList.placeNewPlayer (BUG-03)
- Add time sync (SetTime) during login (BUG-04)
- Fix simulation_distance to use server config, not client view_distance (BUG-05)
- Add movement broadcast with delta-encoded pos/rot packets (BUG-06)
- Add EntityEvent packet (0x22) for permission level during login (BUG-07)
- Fix pack_angle to delegate to pack_degrees for correct negative angle wrapping
- Use classify_move/encode_delta for safe delta encoding (no i16 overflow)
- Reorder post-login packets: WorldBorder→Commands→Time→SpawnPos→Weather
- Move inventory send after chunks (vanilla initInventoryMenu order)

commit.message:
feat: add volsync operator and backup component (#691)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: Windows test failures in config command and agents compiler (#410)

Fix 7 pre-existing Windows CI test failures:

- test_config_command.py (5 failures): PermissionError [WinError 32] when
  TemporaryDirectory cleanup runs while CWD is still inside it. Windows
  holds a handle on the CWD directory. Fix: os.chdir() back to original
  dir inside try/finally before the TemporaryDirectory context exits.

- test_agents_compiler_coverage.py (2 failures): Path separator mismatch.
  relative_to() returns Path with OS-native separators (backslash on
  Windows), but assertions check for forward slashes. Fix: use .as_posix()
  in production code for consistent cross-platform summary output.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: Naimmo <samuel.haddar@baxenergy.com>

perf(ci): comprehensive pipeline optimization (#407)

* Initial plan

* perf(ci): comprehensive pipeline optimization + Docker image publishing

O1: Remove setup-node from test/build/smoke-test jobs
O2: Enable native uv caching (remove actions/cache@v3)
O3: Merge test+build → build-and-test, macOS as root nodes
O7: CI circular dependency bypass in report-status
O8: PR traceability annotation in ci-integration.yml
O9: Decouple live inference from release pipeline, create ci-runtime.yml
Docker: Add Dockerfile + publish-docker job for ghcr.io (issue #326)

Co-authored-by: danielmeppiel <51440732+danielmeppiel@users.noreply.github.com>
Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/microsoft/apm/sessions/6a1c062a-755a-4dcb-8ad9-c1698eeed145

* docs: update CHANGELOG, installation docs, and CI/CD instructions

Add Docker container section to installation docs.
Update CHANGELOG with all pipeline optimizations.
Rewrite cicd.instructions.md for new 4-workflow architecture.
Revert unrelated uv.lock changes.

Co-authored-by: danielmeppiel <51440732+danielmeppiel@users.noreply.github.com>
Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/microsoft/apm/sessions/6a1c062a-755a-4dcb-8ad9-c1698eeed145

* fix: address code review feedback on CI pipeline changes

- Improve circular dependency detection comments and logging
- Add comment explaining Docker latest tag logic
- Fix test count comment in release-validation script
- Clarify CI timing and platform strategy in docs

Co-authored-by: danielmeppiel <51440732+danielmeppiel@users.noreply.github.com>
Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/microsoft/apm/sessions/6a1c062a-755a-4dcb-8ad9-c1698eeed145

* chore: revert unrelated uv.lock format changes

Co-authored-by: danielmeppiel <51440732+danielmeppiel@users.noreply.github.com>
Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/microsoft/apm/sessions/6a1c062a-755a-4dcb-8ad9-c1698eeed145

* refactor(ci): drop Docker publishing, make gh-aw-compat informational

- Remove Dockerfile and .dockerignore — PyInstaller binaries + apm-action
  cover all CI use cases; Docker adds supply chain surface with no user demand
- Remove publish-docker job from build-release.yml
- Make gh-aw-compat continue-on-error: true — non-deterministic external
  dependencies (~4% failure rate) should not block releases. Signal preserved
  via ::warning annotation on failure
- Remove Docker section from installation docs
- Remove Docker references from CI/CD instructions
- Update CHANGELOG

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(ci): remove gh-aw-compat from downstream needs chains

gh-aw-compat is now continue-on-error (informational), so downstream
publish jobs should not wait for it. Remove from publish-pypi,
update-homebrew, and update-scoop needs — gh-aw-compat now runs fully
in parallel with distribution.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* perf(ci): optimize pytest invocations

- Remove -n auto from pyproject.toml addopts (was penalizing single-file
  smoke tests with xdist worker startup overhead)
- Add explicit -n auto --dist worksteal to CI pytest commands (worksteal
  is better than default load distribution for uneven test durations)
- Merge two pytest steps in build-and-test into single invocation
  (saves ~2-3s of interpreter + pytest startup per platform)

All 2386 tests pass.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: address PR review comments

- Remove stale models: read permissions from integration-tests,
  release-validation, and macOS jobs (inference decoupled to ci-runtime.yml)
- Fix bug: GITHUB_CLI_PAT → GITHUB_APM_PAT in dependency test gating
  (test-release-validation.sh:462)
- Fix misleading success summary: remove 'Both README hero scenarios'
  claim when inference tests were skipped; fix GITHUB_TOKEN → GITHUB_APM_PAT
- Remove scripts from ci.yml artifact upload (ci-integration.yml runs
  scripts from default branch, not PR artifacts)
- Add test-release-validation.sh to ci-runtime.yml path filter
- Fix docs: Node.js is also needed in ci-runtime.yml and macOS jobs

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: handle existing .venv in test-integration.sh build_binary()

The build_binary() function called 'uv venv' unconditionally, which fails
when .venv already exists (created by 'uv sync --extra dev' in the CI
workflow step). Apply the same pattern used in install_test_dependencies():
check if .venv exists before creating it.

Also add debug logging to detect_environment() to diagnose artifact
download issues when the binary isn't found.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: restore scripts in ci.yml artifact to preserve path structure

Removing scripts from the upload-artifact path changed the common
ancestor from ./ to ./dist/, causing download-artifact to extract
the binary at ./apm-linux-x86_64/apm instead of ./dist/apm-linux-x86_64/apm.
ci-integration.yml (running from main) expects the dist/ prefix.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------
